expectations.text: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report has an Options Analysis section that is more than 5 lines
expectations.evidence: Options Analysis section spans lines 107-143 — approximately 36 lines including IV environment, flow analysis, strategy reasoning, and a 3-tier strategy table

expectations.text: Report mentions IV Rank or IV Percentile in the options analysis
Report specifically evaluates the put-selling strategy the user asked about
expectations.evidence: Lines 120-141: Detailed put-selling evaluation with 4 reasons to sell puts, plus a 3-tier strategy table (Conservative $15, Moderate $16, Aggressive $17) with delta, DTE, premium, annualized return, and assignment risk

expectations.text: Report recommends for or against selling puts with reasoning tied to IV environment
expectations.evidence: Lines 120-125: 'SELL PUTS because: 1. IV is elevated — you're being paid premium prices for risk that is well-defined by strong fundamentals'

expectations.text: Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit action (BUY/SELL/HOLD or options strategy)
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report has a Valuation Summary section with multiples
expectations.evidence: Lines 49-57: Valuation Summary with P/E TTM 48.2x, P/E FWD 31.4x, P/S 6.6x, P/B 2.3x, PEG 0.79

expectations.text: Report has fundamental analysis providing context for the options play
expectations.evidence: Lines 147-188: Full Fundamental Analysis with Q4/FY2025 highlights, balance sheet, 2026 guidance, competitive position, management

expectations.text: Report has a Sources section with references
Report contains actual current SOFI price data (not fabricated)
expectations.evidence: Line 3: '$18.83 (-28.3% YTD)' — consistent with SOFI data on March 10, 2026

claims.evidence: Consistent with with_skill output and Yahoo Finance source

claims.claim: JPMorgan upgraded to Overweight with $31 PT

claims.evidence: Sourced from Fintel link in sources

suggestions.reason: Old skill produced bonus sections (Risk Summary for Put Sellers, Put-Selling Specific Checklist) that are highly valuable for the user's specific question — no assertion rewards this kind of user-focused adaptation.

suggestions.assertion: Report has an Options Analysis section that is more than 5 lines
suggestions.reason: 'More than 5 lines' is too low a bar. Both outputs have 35-70 lines of options analysis. Raising to 'more than 20 lines' would still pass both but better detect weak implementations.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. The old skill produced an excellent options analysis despite having less specific guidance. The put-selling checklist and risk summary are genuinely useful additions the template didn't require.
